title: Preparing biological samples for metabarcoding
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fj895","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of biological samples (specifically from a marine environment e.g. hatchery or RAS unit) for amplicon sequencing. Starting with a biological sample stored in Qiagen buffer ATL, or similar, it begins with a bead beating process to homogenise the sample. Enzymatic lysis using Metapolyzyme and Proteinase K are emplyed to ensure efficient DNA release. The Qiagen DNeasy kit is used to column extract DNA from lysates. Following concentration estimates of DNA elutions, samples are diluted \u003e1:10 to avoid PCR inhibition during amplicon library preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"11qcj","text":"In every step following enzymatic digestion of samples (and in general), ensure samples are kept at 4C to maximise sample stability. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39rbc","text":"Freeze DNA samples if not being used for \u003e1 week following extraction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"agmhl","text":"Otherwise, storing DNA at 4C in fridge is preferable. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9h6cd","text":"Ensure leaving time for samples to thaw if frozen. Avoid leaving samples thaw for too long as this may lead to degradation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"b395a","text":"Refer to manufacturer's MSDS information for each reagent used to ensure appropriate and safe use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Starting with biological sample (filter, swab, water, biofilm, tissue etc.) stored in Qiagen Buffer ATL (or similar), transfer up to 1 mL to Matrix A bead tube. Buffer ATL Qiagen Catalog #19076 Lysing Matrix A 2 mL tube MP Biomedicals Catalog #SKU 116910050-CF
Perform bead beating in a disruptor at at 5.0 M/s (speed) for 00:00:40 x2 (ensure tube looks somewhat homogenous).
Add 5 µL of Metapolyzyme to each tube and vortex briefly. Incubate samples at 37 °C for 02:00:00 . MetaPolyzyme Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #MAC4L-5MG
Add 20 µL of Proteinase K to each tube, vortex for 00:00:10 , then incubate at 56 °C Overnight . Proteinase K, 100mg Promega Catalog #V3021
Vortex samples for 00:00:15 and centrifuge a 13000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 .
Transfer the supernatant from each tube (up to 900 µL ) into a new tube and centrifuged at 13000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Transfer up to 600 µL of bead-free supernatant to a new 2 mL tube.

Premix 70% ethanol and Qiagen lysis buffer AL 1:1 to add to sample at a ratio of 1:1:1 e.g. for 10 samples of 500 µL each, premix 550 µL of buffer AL and 550 µL of 70% ethanol and add 1 mL of ethanol/buffer AL mixture to each sample. Buffer AL, Lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19076

Hereafter, the manufacturer's protocol for the Qiagen DNeasy Blood and Tissue kit is followed with some modifications: Load < 600 µL of lysate mixture (ATL, AL and EtOH) at a time into the column Spin at 6000 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flow-through. Repeat as necessary until all lysate is loaded on column e.g. mixture of 1500 µL may take x3 initial spins and flow through discarding to complete column binding. QIAgen DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit, 50 rxn Qiagen Catalog #69504
Place the DNeasy Mini spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube (provided), add 500 µL Buffer AW1, and centrifuge a 6000 x g, °C, 1 minute (8000 rpm). Discard flow-through and collection tube.

Place the DNeasy Mini spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube (provided), add 500 µL Buffer AW2, and centrifuge for at 20000 x g, °C, 3 minutes to dry the DNeasy membrane. Discard flow-through and collection tube.
Perform final elution in 100 µL of AE buffer.
Check approximate concentration of extracted DNA using a Nanodrop.
Prepare 1:10 dilution of each extraction for PCR (to avoid PCR inhibition). Perform further dilution of sample to a maximum final concentration of ~ 1 ng/μl - 10 ng/μl
Use ~ 50 ng of DNA in a 20 µL per sequencing library PCR reaction (see amplicon library PCR protocol).
creator: Tim Regan
